task.title: Fix Flaky Rate-Limiter Test
task.description: test_rate_limiter intermittently fails due to unexpected request allowances.
constraints: Avoid running heavy stress load tests.
success_criteria: Identify logic flaw in rate limiter

steps.thought: First, inspect the rate limiter test to understand what conditions it expects.
steps.observation: Test tries 10 requests within 1 second and expects the last 2 to be blocked.

steps.thought: Open rate limiter implementation to check the underlying logic.
steps.observation: Limiter tracks timestamps but does linear scan over history.

steps.thought: Re-run the test once to see the exact failure.
steps.observation: Test intermittently fails: allowed more requests than intended.

steps.thought: Run a controlled simulation with 20 requests, staying under the limit.
steps.observation: Observed inconsistent blocking on higher timestamps.

steps.thought: Examine timestamp cleanup logic, suspecting stale entries aren't removed properly.
steps.observation: Old timestamps removed only when array length > window_size, not when expired.

steps.thought: Fix logic: remove timestamps older than 1-second window on each call.

arguments.new_str: self.history = [t for t in self.history if now - t <= 1]

steps.thought: Run the test suite again to confirm fix.

arguments.new_str: Rate limiter failed due to stale timestamps accumulating; fixed by purging outdated entries each call.

steps.thought: Final sanity check on limiter code.
